# Xelo — AI SBOM Generator by NuGuard AI

Xelo is an open-source AI Bill of Materials (AI SBOM) generator for agentic and
LLM-powered applications. It scans Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Jupyter notebooks,
SQL schemas, Dockerfiles, and IaC files to produce a structured graph of every AI
component — agents, models, tools, prompts, datastores, guardrails, auth nodes,
and deployment infrastructure. Output is a machine-readable JSON document you can
validate, version-control, and feed into your security toolchain.

Xelo is fully offline by default — no API key, no external calls, no data leaves
your network. LLM enrichment is an optional third phase gated behind `--llm`.

License: Apache-2.0 · Built by NuGuard AI (https://nuguard.ai/)

---

## What Xelo detects

Xelo runs a 3-phase extraction pipeline:

1. **AST-aware adapters** — language-specific parsers for Python (`ast`) and
   TypeScript (tree-sitter) extract framework-specific components with high precision.
2. **Regex fallbacks** — model names, auth keywords, secret patterns, datastores,
   and IaC signals caught across all file types (SQL, YAML, Dockerfiles, Nginx, prompts).
3. **LLM enrichment** (optional) — verifies uncertain detections, enriches node
   descriptions, generates a use-case summary and IaC security briefing.

**Component types detected:** AGENT, MODEL, TOOL, PROMPT, DATASTORE, GUARDRAIL,
AUTH, DEPLOYMENT, CONTAINER, VECTOR_STORE, EMBEDDING, PIPELINE, CHAIN.

**Supported AI frameworks (Python):** LangChain, LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK,
CrewAI (code + YAML), AutoGen (code + YAML), Google ADK, LlamaIndex, Agno,
AWS BedrockAgentCore, Azure AI Agent Service, Guardrails AI, MCP Server (FastMCP
and low-level), Azure Semantic Kernel.

**Supported AI frameworks (TypeScript/JavaScript):** LangChain.js, LangGraph.js,
OpenAI Agents (TS), Azure AI Agents (TS), Agno (TS), MCP Server (TS).

**Infrastructure & IaC:** Terraform, CloudFormation, Azure Bicep, Kubernetes
manifests, GCP Deployment Manager, GitHub Actions, Dockerfiles, Nginx configs.

**Data & Storage:** SQL schemas with PHI/PII classification, SQLAlchemy models,
Django models, Pydantic models, prompt files (.txt / .md / .jinja).

---

## Security & compliance

Xelo includes a structural vulnerability scanner (21 XELO rules) derived from
OWASP AI Top 10, NIST AI RMF, and data protection frameworks — no network access required.

Rule categories:
- **AI graph rules (XELO-001–009):** missing guardrails, regulated data to external LLMs,
  voice + regulated data exposure, prompt injection risk, auth coverage gaps.
- **IaC / Encryption (XELO-010–013):** regulated data workloads without encryption at rest,
  no CMK/BYOK, secrets in env vars, missing secret management.
- **IAM & Identity (XELO-014–017):** overly permissive IAM with regulated data in scope, cluster-scoped
  roles without permission boundary, GHA without OIDC.
- **Resilience (XELO-018–020):** single-AZ deployment with regulated data, no health checks,
  containers without resource limits.
- **Container security (XELO-021):** containers running as root.

---

## Output formats

- `json` — Xelo-native `AiSbomDocument` JSON (Pydantic v2, JSON Schema available)
- `cyclonedx` — AI components as CycloneDX 1.6 BOM
- `unified` — standard deps BOM merged with AI-BOM via `AiBomMerger`

---

## Toolbox plugins

Run any plugin with: `xelo plugin run <name> sbom.json`

| Plugin            | Purpose                                                   | Network? |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|----------|
| vulnerability     | 21 structural VLA rules — fully offline                   | Offline  |
| atlas             | Maps findings to MITRE ATLAS v2 techniques                | Offline  |
| sarif             | Exports findings as SARIF 2.1.0                           | Offline  |
| cyclonedx         | Exports AI nodes as CycloneDX 1.6 BOM                     | Offline  |
| markdown          | Human-readable Markdown report                            | Offline  |
| license           | SPDX licence compliance check                             | Offline  |
| dependency        | Dependency graph analysis and freshness scoring           | Offline  |
| ghas              | Upload SARIF to GitHub Advanced Security                  | Network  |
| aws-security-hub  | Push findings to AWS Security Hub (ASFF)                  | Network  |
| xray              | Submit SBOM to JFrog Xray                                 | Network  |

---

## Platform & Overview

- [Homepage](https://nuguard.ai/) — Product overview, hero demo, personas, and quickstart.
- [Getting Started](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=getting-started) — Install, first scan, LLM enrichment, supported frameworks.
- [CLI Reference](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=cli-reference) — Full command and flag matrix for `xelo scan`, `xelo validate`, `xelo schema`, `xelo plugin`.
- [Developer Guide](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=developer-guide) — Python library API, toolbox plugins, provider config examples.
- [AI SBOM Schema](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=aibom-schema) — Every field — node types, metadata, evidence, edges, data classification, ScanSummary.
- [Vulnerability Scanning](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=vulnerability-scanning) — All 21 VLA rules, remediation steps, and a worked healthcare voice agent example.
- [Troubleshooting](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=troubleshooting) — Common errors and remediation.
- [Changelog](https://nuguard.ai/doc.html?page=CHANGELOG) — User-facing changes per release.

---

## Quickstart

```bash
# Install
pip install xelo

# Scan a local repository (fully offline)
xelo scan ./my-ai-app --output sbom.json

# Scan a remote repository
xelo scan https://github.com/org/repo --ref main

# Validate the output
xelo validate sbom.json

# Run structural security rules (21 VLA rules, no network)
xelo plugin run vulnerability sbom.json

# Export to CycloneDX 1.6
xelo scan ./my-ai-app --format cyclonedx --output bom.cdx.json

# Enable optional LLM enrichment
xelo scan ./my-ai-app --llm --output sbom.json
```

Python API:

```python
from pathlib import Path
from xelo import AiSbomConfig, AiSbomExtractor, AiSbomSerializer

doc = AiSbomExtractor().extract_from_path(
    path=Path("./my-ai-app"),
    config=AiSbomConfig(),
)
print(f"nodes={len(doc.nodes)}, edges={len(doc.edges)}")
json_str = AiSbomSerializer.to_json(doc)
```

---
